title: Comprehensive Security Scan Review and Remediation
description:
Review all high-priority items from the Salesforce security certification scan and address or document each finding. For items not on `app.DataToolsPro.com`, note that they are not applicable.

## Findings Reviewed

1. **Path Traversal on /report-error** — Fixed: implemented input validation on the report-error endpoint.
2. **SQL Injection on /report-error** — False positive: no database queries in the function.
3. **SQL Injection (Hypersonic) on /cookie** — Already using parameterized queries.
4. **SQL Injection (MySQL) on /salesforce/callback** — False positive: Eloquent ORM provides automatic parameterization.
5. **SQL Injection (Oracle) on /get-erd-views-option** — False positive: uses Eloquent `ErdView::all()` with no user input.
6. **SQL Injection (PostgreSQL) on /salesforce/redirect** — False positive: endpoint only redirects to Salesforce.
7. **SQL Injection (SQLite) on /cookie** — Already using required protections.
8. **CSP Wildcard** — Additional domains added to CSP.
9. **Missing frame-ancestors** — Added frame-ancestors to CSP.
10. **unsafe-inline in CSP** — Known issue tied to a third-party library; deferred.
11. **Strict-Transport-Security missing** — Added HSTS header.
12. **Application Error Disclosure** — False positive: custom error messages in use.
13. **Cookie No HttpOnly Flag** — Already set to HttpOnly=true.
14. **Cookie Without Secure Flag** — Already encrypted and secure.
15. **X-Content-Type-Options missing** — Added `nosniff` header.
16. **Timestamp Disclosure** — From Pendo CDN (third-party); headers added as mitigation.
acceptance_criteria: All high-priority scan findings are reviewed and documented
Path traversal on /report-error is fixed with input validation
False positive SQL injection findings are documented with code evidence
HSTS header is added to all responses
frame-ancestors directive is added to CSP
middleware_changes: Multiple security headers added via middleware: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, frame-ancestors in CSP. Path traversal fix applied to /report-error endpoint. Input validation strengthened.
implementation_notes: Comprehensive review of 16+ scan findings. Most SQL injection alerts were false positives due to Laravel Eloquent ORM. Path traversal on /report-error was a genuine finding that was fixed. Security headers (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, frame-ancestors) were added. The unsafe-inline CSP issue was deferred due to a third-party library dependency. Cookie security was confirmed as already compliant.
comment_summary: Developer methodically reviewed each scan finding, providing code evidence for false positives and implementing fixes for genuine issues. Path traversal was fixed with input validation. Multiple SQL injection findings were documented as false positives due to Eloquent ORM usage. Security headers (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, frame-ancestors) were added. The unsafe-inline CSP issue was flagged as requiring more time due to a third-party library dependency.
